queijadas-de-sintra-teleculinaria.name: Queijadas De Sintra

ingredients.item: 500 g de queijo fresco sem sal

instructions: In a bowl, mix the flour with the water, knead very well and then extend the dough with the roll until you get about 1 mm thick.
Cut the dough into circles and line small shapes, previously greased with butter, with them. Have them on a board and let it dry a little.
In a bowl, mix the cheese with the yolks and sugar and beat it well. Add the flour and cinnamon and mix again.
Distribute the filling by the forms, without filling them too much, and take them in the oven, preheated to 180oC, for about 25 to 30 minutes. Take it off, let it cool down and unshape it.

quesadillas-with-squash-blossoms.name: Quesadillas With Squash Blossoms
instructions: Heat oil in a large skillet, preferably cast iron, over medium. Cook onion and garlic, stirring occasionally, until very soft and golden, 10–12 minutes; season with salt. Transfer to a small bowl and wipe skillet clean.

Toast a tortilla in the same skillet over medium-high until golden brown but not crunchy on one side, about 1 minute. Turn tortilla over and scatter one-quarter of cheese over one half of toasted side. Arrange 2 squash blossoms and one-quarter of onion mixture on top of cheese and fold tortilla in half to create a half-moon. Press down on it lightly to help tortilla adhere. Continue cooking, turning once or twice and pressing occasionally, until cheese is melted and tortilla begins to brown and crisp in spots (turn down the heat if needed), about 3 minutes. Transfer quesadilla to a plate. Repeat with remaining tortillas, cheese, squash blossoms, and onion mixture to make 3 more quesadillas.

quiche-lorraine-with-kourou-dough-argiro.name: Quiche Lorraine With Kourou Dough

ingredients.item: Some butter and flour for the form
Some butter and flour for the form

instructions: Shake the flour on the work bench.
Sprinkle with salt and rub the frozen butter on the flour.
With the fingertips rub the mixture until it acquires a texture reminiscent of wet sand.
Add the cold cream cheese and combine the ingredients with the hands until dough is formed.
Shape it into a tray, wrap it with membrane and refrigerate for 30 minutes to tighten.
On a flowered bench, with a dick cooker and flouring lightly and the surface of the dough, spread a sheet about 3–4 mm kick and about 35 cm in diameter.
We'll use a form with a detachable base diameter of 23 cm.
Butter and flour the form and shake the excess flour. An anti-stick paper only at the base of the form will help with easy disforming.
With the help of the rolling machine, transfer the dough to the form, making sure it sticks out a little perimeter.
We drill the base with a fork.
Place non-stick paper on the dough and fill with legumes or rice for weight.
Bake in a well preheated evening in the air, at 180°C, for 20 minutes.
Remove the weights and paper carefully.
At this stage, brush the base with egg white and put the dough back in the oven for 13–15 minutes, to seal and bake mostly.
Leave it on the counter until we prepare the filling.
The dough can also be made in the mixer with the cutters, using the intermittent function for about 30 seconds.
